text: Recommendations for the definition and interpretation of halogen bonds1 This recommendation provides definition of the term “halogen bond” which has been used, in broad senses, to describe non-covalent interactions in which a halogen atom acts as an electrophile. A halogen bond is described as an attraction between an electron density concentration in a halogen within a molecular entity acting as the halogen bond donor and an electron density depletion within the same or a different molecular entity, acting as the halogen bond acceptor. In order to aid in the characterization of halogen bonds, the general architecture of the R–X···Y components and common R–X and Y molecules are listed. Criteria for experimental and theoretical characterization of halogen bonds include the high directionality associated with the halogen bond interaction, together with short interatomic distances (less than 3.5 times the average van der Waals radius for the interacting atoms), as well as characteristic NMR shifts, IR bands and UV–vis absorptions. The aim of this definition is to introduce a systematic terminology for the description of halogen bonds and their role in supramolecular systems and crystal engineering.
headline: Fully Human Written
